title: cerastium glabratum
intro: Cerastium glabratum, also known as mouse-ear chickweed, is a perennial plant species belonging to the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae). Traditionally, it has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and promoting wound healing. Cerastium glabratum can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and salves, allowing its medicinal properties to be harnessed for therapeutic purposes.
